response: Japan, a nation of approximately 125 million people, boasts a substantial economy with a GDP of $4.2 trillion, though growth remains modest at 1.1%, while unemployment stays remarkably low at 2.6% and inflation sits at 3.2%. The country carries a notably high public debt-to-GDP ratio of 263%, yet maintains strong trade activity with $756 billion in exports. Japan also stands out for its exceptional quality of life, reflected in one of the world's highest life expectancies of 84.6 years.
